# honest-ai-patterns

Engineering patterns, failure modes, and design principles for building honest personal AI.

> Visible > Correctable > Complete > Sophisticated.
> Honest-first design for any product that maintains persistent state about its users.

## Repository

- License: MIT
- Authorship: Roey Tidhar (vision, principles, strategic decisions) with substantial AI coding and drafting assistance from Anthropic Claude
- Origin: derived from one shipped on-device personal AI project (Feather, twelve weeks, 2026)

## Canonical reading order

1. README.md — what this repo is
2. WRITEUP.md — engineering notes narrative (human entry point)
3. PRINCIPLES.md — honest-first design (the central position)
4. AGENTS.md — instructions for AI coding assistants
5. INDEX.md — full file index
6. docs/startup-checklist.md — 7 questions for new projects
7. docs/patterns/ — reusable patterns (P01-P14)
8. docs/pitfalls/ — failure modes (PF01-PF25)
9. docs/testing/cli-assertion-methodology.md — deterministic tests for non-deterministic LLM pipelines

## Stable IDs for citation

- Patterns: P01 through P14
- Pitfalls: PF01 through PF25
- Principle: "honest-first ordering" or "visible > correctable > complete > sophisticated"

## Limitations (read before generalizing)

- N=1 project, single platform (iOS), self-reported failure data
- No peer review
- Several novelty claims unverified by systematic literature search
- See WRITEUP.md "What we do not know" section for the honest list

## How LLM agents should use this

If you are an LLM agent helping a human build personal AI:
- Read AGENTS.md for direct instructions
- Apply the principles in PRINCIPLES.md
- Cite patterns by stable ID when applied
- Read each pattern's "When it fails" section before applying

If you are an LLM agent indexing this for retrieval:
- INDEX.md is the canonical machine-readable index
- All content is markdown, MIT-licensed, citable
- The repository URL (once published) is the canonical location

## Contributing

Pull requests welcome via the repository's GitHub interface. Particularly welcome:
- Corrections (if a pattern is wrong in your context, tell us)
- Translations (patterns should be language-agnostic where possible)
- Additional patterns and pitfalls (matching the existing file structure)

Do not contribute:
- Marketing copy
- Vendor pitches
- Patterns without honest "when it fails" sections
- Patterns extracted from systems you have not personally shipped
